defects.defect_text: Compressor cylinder liner shows wear with reduced compression efficiency and oil carryover.
Compressor valve plate cracked or chipped, producing backflow and pressure drop.
Main bearing running hot with discoloration, elevated vibration and noise.
Air leakage at shaft seal observed as hissing noise and loss of delivered pressure.
Intercooler/aftercooler blocked with scale/dirt causing high discharge air temperature.
Air intake filter clogged with dust resulting in reduced flow and overheating.
Lubrication pump failure causing oil starvation to compressor internals and abnormal wear.
Drive motor overloaded or tripping during compression cycles with audible humming.
Excessive compressor vibration due to imbalance or loose mounts.
Pressure sensor/transducer failure resulting in incorrect load/unload operation.

subsystems.description: Air compressor main unit including cylinders, pistons, rotors or screws depending on design.
Electric motor driving the compressor via coupling or belt.
Lubrication system for bearings, cylinders and screws including pump and reservoir.
Intercooler/aftercooler and cooling fans or water circuits for compressed air temperature control.
Control system including pressure switches, sensors, unloaders and safety shutdowns.
Air intake filter and oil separators ensuring clean air supply.

components.description: Compressor cylinder or compression chamber housing pistons or screws.
Suction and discharge valves regulating airflow in reciprocating compressors.
Main and connecting rod bearings supporting rotating assemblies.
Shaft and piston seals preventing leakage of compressed air or oil.
Air-cooled or water-cooled heat exchangers reducing discharge temperature.
Air intake filter element preventing dust ingress; oil separator filter removing oil mist.
Oil pump supplying lubrication to bearings and moving compressor parts.
Electric motor providing torque to compressor shaft.
Flexible/mechanical coupling or belt connecting motor to compressor shaft.
Pressure, temperature and vibration sensors providing feedback to control system.

defect_types.description: Material loss on compressor cylinders, rings or rotors due to friction.
Fracture in valves, housings or components from fatigue or overload.
Unintended air or oil leakage at seals, gaskets or fittings.
Excessive temperature rise in bearings, cylinders or discharge air.
Restriction due to dust/oil clogging intake or separator filters.
Functional failure of pump, separator, or drive component.
Excessive oscillation or imbalance affecting bearings and mounts.
Failure of pressure, temperature or control sensors affecting operation.
Obstruction in intercoolers, aftercoolers or oil lines restricting flow.
Excessive electrical or mechanical load tripping drive motor or reducing performance.

meta.machine: Air Compressor (used with Pilger HSP Machine)

sources.title: Operation and Maintenance of Reciprocating Compressors
Case study: Reliability issues in reciprocating compressors

meta.notes: Air compressors are auxiliary systems supporting Pilger HSP machines, commonly for pneumatic clamping, actuation and tool change. Failures in compressors directly impact uptime. The taxonomy covers reciprocating and screw types typically used in metal forming plants.
